title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses how to distinguish genuine product-market fit from delusion. He argues that rabid fans and formal customer interviews are unreliable signals, and instead advocates for embedded customer relationships and assessing whether customers actively pull the product rather than requiring sales push.
writeup: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, breaks down how founders misread product-market fit on the EO channel. His central claim: rabid users are not proof of real PMF, since intense passion from a small segment is easily mistaken for broad demand. He frames true PMF as demand pull rather than push, and argues it should be gauged through embedded customer relationships instead of formal interviews. He also advises building toward where AI models are heading rather than their current capabilities.
